agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"external-inventory-api","status":"risk","issue":"No timeout configured on outbound HTTP call. A hung upstream connection will block the sync indefinitely, potentially overlapping with the next 5-minute interval and causing cascading resource exhaustion."},{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"local-database","status":"risk","issue":"If the external API returns malformed or partial data due to a degraded upstream, there is no validation gate before writing to the local DB. Stock counts could be zeroed or corrupted silently."},{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"cron-scheduler","status":"risk","issue":"No mutual exclusion or overlap detection. If a sync run hangs (no timeout), the next scheduled run at T+5m can start concurrently, leading to duplicate writes or DB contention."}],"contract_compliance":"Unknown — no schema validation or contract testing mentioned between this service and the external inventory API. A breaking upstream change would propagate silently.","data_flow_integrity":"At risk. The pipeline is external API → service → local DB with no intermediate validation, no idempotency guarantees, and no checksumming of sync results. A partial sync is indistinguishable from a full sync."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No structured logging — raw console.log produces unstructured text that is difficult to filter, aggregate, or alert on in any log management system (Datadog, ELK, CloudWatch).","No log levels — success and failure both use console.log instead of info/error, making it impossible to filter by severity.","No contextual fields — missing sync run ID, record count synced, duration, API response status code, or timestamp. An on-call engineer at 3am cannot determine WHAT failed or HOW MUCH data was affected.","No logging of partial success — if 950 of 1000 products sync but 50 fail, there is no record of the partial outcome.","Error logging captures only err.message, discarding the stack trace, HTTP status code, and response body needed for root cause analysis."]},"metrics":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No sync duration metric (histogram) — cannot detect gradual upstream degradation.","No sync success/failure counter — cannot alert on error rate or build an SLO.","No records-synced gauge — cannot detect a sync that 'succeeds' but returns 0 records (silent data loss).","No API response time metric — cannot distinguish service issues from upstream latency.","No active sync gauge — cannot detect overlapping runs."]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagated to the external API call.","No span covering the DB write phase — cannot isolate whether latency is from the API call or the DB write."]},"debugging_capability":"Severely limited. An on-call engineer would see only 'sync failed: connection timeout' with no context about which run, how many records were affected, what the upstream returned, or how long the call took. Debugging requires SSH-ing into the container and adding ad-hoc logging — unacceptable for production."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"External inventory API is down","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, base 2s, jitter). After retries exhausted, log structured error at ERROR level with attempt count and emit a failure metric."},{"scenario":"External inventory API is slow or hung","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a 30-second timeout on the outbound HTTP call. This is the single highest-priority fix — without it, a hung connection can exhaust the connection pool and block all future syncs indefinitely."},{"scenario":"External inventory API returns 200 but empty or malformed payload","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response schema and record count before writing to DB. If the upstream returns zero products when the DB has thousands, treat it as an anomaly and abort the sync rather than zeroing all stock."},{"scenario":"Database is unavailable during write phase","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Wrap DB writes in a transaction. On failure, log the error with full context and emit a metric. Consider a dead-letter mechanism for failed batches."},{"scenario":"Concurrent sync runs overlap due to previous run not completing","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement a mutex lock (e.g., advisory DB lock or in-memory flag) to prevent overlapping runs. If a lock is held, skip the run and log a warning."},{"scenario":"Partial sync — some products update, others fail mid-batch","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Use a database transaction for atomicity. Either the entire batch commits or none of it does. Log the boundary clearly."}],"retry_config":"None. Must add: 3 retries, exponential backoff starting at 2s, with jitter to avoid thundering herd.","timeout_config":"None. CRITICAL gap. Must add: 30s connect timeout, 60s read timeout on outbound API call.","circuit_breakers":"None. Acceptable for now given the 5-minute polling interval — the interval itself acts as a crude backoff. Nice-to-have for v2 but not a ship-blocker if retries and timeouts are added.","graceful_degradation":"None. If sync fails, stale stock counts persist silently. At minimum, a staleness metric (time since last successful sync) must be emitted so downstream consumers and alerts can react."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Re-deploy previous container image. No database migrations in this release, so rollback is clean and safe.","monitoring_coverage":"None. Zero metrics, no health endpoint, no alerting. The team will not know this service is failing until a customer reports incorrect stock counts — potentially hours or days later.","on_call_documentation":"Missing. No runbook exists for: sync failures, upstream API degradation, stale data alerts, or how to manually trigger a sync.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["INVENTORY_API_TIMEOUT_MS","INVENTORY_API_RETRY_COUNT","INVENTORY_API_RETRY_BASE_MS"],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Metrics exporter endpoint configuration (e.g., StatsD, Prometheus scrape target)","Health check endpoint registration in load balancer or orchestrator"],"infra_provisioning":["Alerting rules for: sync_failure_count > 0 for 15m, sync_staleness > 10m, sync_duration_p99 > 45s"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema changes in this release. Writes are to existing stock count columns. Rollback does not require data migration."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No timeout on outbound API call","reason":"A hung upstream connection will block the sync process indefinitely. In the worst case, connection pool exhaustion cascades to block all future sync runs with no visibility. This is a production safety issue, not a nice-to-have.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Zero metrics, no health endpoint, no structured logging","reason":"The service has no way to signal its own health. The team will discover failures only through customer-reported symptoms (wrong stock counts). An on-call engineer cannot diagnose, scope, or resolve issues without metrics, structured logs, and a health endpoint. Minimum viable: sync success/failure counter, sync duration histogram, last-successful-sync gauge, /health endpoint, and structured JSON logging with severity levels.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No response validation or anomaly detection on upstream data","reason":"If the external inventory API returns an empty array or malformed data, the service will overwrite valid stock counts with zeros or garbage. This is a silent data corruption vector with customer-facing impact. Must validate payload schema and record count before committing to the database.","routed_to":"Shaq"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Timeout and retry gaps are clear and unambiguous","Observability is objectively absent","Rollback path is clean given no migrations","Failure modes are well-understood for this sync-poll pattern"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact DB write semantics — assumed non-transactional based on description but could not verify code","Overlap behavior depends on runtime (Node single-thread vs. worker threads) which was not specified"],"assumptions":["Service runs on a single instance or has no distributed coordination","The 5-minute schedule is a simple setInterval or cron, not a distributed scheduler with built-in deduplication","Database writes are not wrapped in a transaction","No rate limiting exists on the external API that would complicate retry logic"]}}
